diagnostics.message: Required accepted P2 or namespace evidence is missing.
Required upstream evidence is not passing.
Required upstream evidence bytes do not match the immutable package.
Accepted fixed-namespace evidence is missing.
Accepted fixed-namespace evidence is not passing.
Accepted fixed-namespace evidence bytes do not match the immutable package.
The authority manifest or declaration does not match its checked hash closure.
The same identity declaration is review-required, so no normalization occurs.
The identity mapping does not match the immutable package.
The immutable component-identity package is inconsistent with its checked closure.
A candidate source byte differs from the immutable package.
The exact 22 checked identity substitutions were not all applied.
The fixed TeamButton to Button identity mapping was reversed.
Identity output differs from the accepted fixed-namespace input baseline.
The Stage-2 namespace input hashes differ from the Stage-1 identity output hashes.
The fixed identity stage was skipped before namespace normalization.
The accepted fixed-namespace stage was skipped before compilation.
Namespace normalization was run before the fixed identity stage.
A checked identity pointer contains a value outside the one accepted TeamButton relation.
The mapping requests an additional pointer outside the exact 22 substitutions.
Identity normalization changed a narrative Button mention.
Identity normalization touched a sixth file outside the five-file authority closure.
The accepted namespace descriptor bytes differ from the identity package.
The accepted namespace package bytes differ from the identity package.
The exported namespace normalizer implementation bytes differ from the identity package.
Checked compiler, normalizer, proof, or runtime bytes differ from the immutable package.
The descriptor requests an arbitrary identity, namespace, layout, component, fact, policy, or executable action.
The unchanged source-conformance compiler failed in the isolated chained workspace.
Persisted chained compiler artifacts fail exact baseline or post-temporary-workspace integrity.
The unchanged compiler rejects a source fact that expands accepted P2 authority.
Final component-identity evidence does not match its complete checked closure.
